annotations.label: Quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: a quadrilateral is a four-sided polygon

annotations.note: Mathlib defines a general n-vertex `Polygon` structure (a quadrilateral would be `Polygon P 4`), but has no dedicated quadrilateral definition.

annotations.label: Simple vs. complex quadrilaterals

anchor.snippet: Quadrilaterals are either simple (not self-intersecting), or complex

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of a self-intersecting versus simple polygon.

annotations.label: Interior angle sum of a quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: The interior angles of a simple (and planar ) quadrilateral ABCD add up to 360 degrees

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the triangle angle sum but has no quadrilateral/polygon interior-angle-sum theorem.

annotations.label: Special case of n-gon angle sum

anchor.snippet: This is a special case of the n -gon interior angle sum formula

annotations.note: No general n-gon interior-angle-sum formula exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quadrilaterals tile the plane

anchor.snippet: All non-self-crossing quadrilaterals tile the plane

annotations.note: Mathlib has no tiling-of-the-plane results for quadrilaterals.

annotations.label: Simple quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: Any quadrilateral that is not self-intersecting is a simple quadrilateral.

annotations.note: No self-intersection / simple-polygon concept is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convex quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: In a convex quadrilateral all interior angles are less than 180°

annotations.note: Mathlib has general set convexity but no convex-quadrilateral definition via interior angles.

annotations.label: Irregular quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: no sides are parallel

annotations.note: No quadrilateral-classification definitions exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trapezium / trapezoid

anchor.snippet: at least one pair of opposite sides are parallel

annotations.note: Mathlib has no trapezoid definition.

annotations.label: Isosceles trapezoid

anchor.snippet: one pair of opposite sides are parallel and the base angles are equal in measure

annotations.note: Mathlib has no isosceles-trapezoid definition.

annotations.label: Parallelogram

anchor.snippet: a quadrilateral with two pairs of parallel sides

annotations.note: Mathlib has the parallelogram law as a norm identity but no geometric parallelogram-shape definition.

annotations.label: Equivalent conditions for parallelogram

anchor.snippet: Equivalent conditions are that opposite sides are of equal length

annotations.note: No parallelogram characterization theorem exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rhombus

anchor.snippet: all four sides are of equal length (equilateral)

annotations.note: Mathlib has no rhombus definition.

annotations.label: Equivalent condition for rhombus

anchor.snippet: An equivalent condition is that the diagonals perpendicularly bisect each other

annotations.note: The orthogonality of equal-side diagonals follows from this perpendicular-bisector lemma, but rhombus itself is undefined.

annotations.label: Rhomboid

anchor.snippet: a parallelogram in which adjacent sides are of unequal lengths

annotations.note: Mathlib has no rhomboid definition.

annotations.label: Rectangle

anchor.snippet: all four angles are right angles (equiangular)

annotations.note: Mathlib has no rectangle definition.

annotations.label: Equivalent condition for rectangle

anchor.snippet: An equivalent condition is that the diagonals bisect each other, and are equal in length

annotations.note: No rectangle characterization theorem exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Square

anchor.snippet: all four sides are of equal length (equilateral), and all four angles are right angles

annotations.note: Mathlib has no square (shape) definition.

annotations.label: Square iff rhombus and rectangle

anchor.snippet: A quadrilateral is a square if and only if it is both a rhombus and a rectangle

annotations.note: These shape types are not defined in Mathlib, so the characterization is absent.

annotations.label: Oblong

anchor.snippet: longer than wide, or wider than long

annotations.note: Mathlib has no oblong definition.

annotations.label: Kite

anchor.snippet: two pairs of adjacent sides are of equal length

annotations.note: Mathlib has no kite definition.

annotations.label: Kite diagonal properties

anchor.snippet: This implies that one diagonal divides the kite into congruent triangles

annotations.note: The underlying perpendicular-bisector orthogonality is in Mathlib (and noted there as the kite-diagonal fact), but kites and the congruence claim are not formalized.

annotations.label: Tangential quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: the four sides are tangents to an inscribed circle

annotations.note: Mathlib has no tangential-quadrilateral / inscribed-circle-quadrilateral definition.

annotations.label: Pitot characterization of tangential quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: A convex quadrilateral is tangential if and only if opposite sides have equal sums

annotations.note: Pitot's theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tangential trapezoid

anchor.snippet: a trapezoid where the four sides are tangents to an inscribed circle

annotations.note: Mathlib has no tangential-trapezoid definition.

annotations.label: Cyclic quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: the four vertices lie on a circumscribed circle

annotations.note: `Concyclic`/`Cospherical` capture four points lying on a common circle, but there is no bundled cyclic-quadrilateral concept.

annotations.label: Cyclic iff opposite angles sum to 180°

anchor.snippet: A convex quadrilateral is cyclic if and only if opposite angles sum to 180°

annotations.note: Both directions are formalized via oriented angles mod π (forward here; converse is `cospherical_of_two_zsmul_oangle_eq_of_not_collinear`).

annotations.label: Right kite

anchor.snippet: a kite with two opposite right angles

annotations.note: Mathlib has no right-kite definition.

annotations.label: Harmonic quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: a cyclic quadrilateral such that the products of the lengths of the opposing sides are equal

annotations.note: Mathlib has no harmonic-quadrilateral definition.

annotations.label: Bicentric quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: it is both tangential and cyclic

annotations.note: Mathlib has no bicentric-quadrilateral definition.

annotations.label: Orthodiagonal quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: the diagonals cross at right angles

annotations.note: Mathlib has no orthodiagonal-quadrilateral definition.

annotations.label: Equidiagonal quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: the diagonals are of equal length

annotations.note: Mathlib has no equidiagonal-quadrilateral definition.

annotations.label: Bisect-diagonal quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: one diagonal bisects the other into equal lengths

annotations.note: Mathlib has no bisect-diagonal-quadrilateral definition.

annotations.label: Ex-tangential quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: the four extensions of the sides are tangent to an excircle

annotations.note: Mathlib has no ex-tangential-quadrilateral definition.

annotations.label: Equilic quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: has two opposite equal sides that when extended, meet at 60°

annotations.note: Mathlib has no equilic-quadrilateral definition.

annotations.label: Watt quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: A Watt quadrilateral is a quadrilateral with a pair of opposite sides of equal length

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Watt-quadrilateral definition.

annotations.label: Quadric quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: A quadric quadrilateral is a convex quadrilateral whose four vertices all lie on the perimeter of a square

annotations.note: Mathlib has no quadric-quadrilateral definition.

annotations.label: Diametric quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: A diametric quadrilateral is a cyclic quadrilateral having one of its sides as a diameter of the circumcircle

annotations.note: Mathlib has no diametric-quadrilateral definition.

annotations.label: Hjelmslev quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: A Hjelmslev quadrilateral is a quadrilateral with two right angles at opposite vertices

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Hjelmslev-quadrilateral definition.

annotations.label: Concave quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: In a concave quadrilateral, one interior angle is bigger than 180°

annotations.note: Mathlib has no concave-quadrilateral definition.

annotations.label: Dart (arrowhead)

anchor.snippet: A dart (or arrowhead) is a concave quadrilateral with bilateral symmetry like a kite

annotations.note: Mathlib has no dart/arrowhead definition.

annotations.label: Crossed (complex) quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: A self-intersecting quadrilateral is called variously a cross-quadrilateral

annotations.note: Mathlib has no self-intersecting-quadrilateral concept.

annotations.label: Crossed quadrilateral interior angle sum

anchor.snippet: add up to 720°

annotations.note: No angle-sum result for crossed quadrilaterals exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Crossed trapezoid

anchor.snippet: a crossed quadrilateral in which one pair of nonadjacent sides is parallel

annotations.note: Mathlib has no crossed-trapezoid definition.

annotations.label: Antiparallelogram

anchor.snippet: a crossed quadrilateral in which each pair of nonadjacent sides have equal lengths

annotations.note: Mathlib has no antiparallelogram definition.

annotations.label: Crossed rectangle

anchor.snippet: an antiparallelogram whose sides are two opposite sides and the two diagonals of a rectangle

annotations.note: Mathlib has no crossed-rectangle definition.

annotations.label: Crossed square

anchor.snippet: a special case of a crossed rectangle where two of the sides intersect at right angles

annotations.note: Mathlib has no crossed-square definition.

annotations.label: Diagonals

anchor.snippet: The two diagonals of a convex quadrilateral are the line segments that connect opposite vertices

annotations.note: No quadrilateral-diagonal definition exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bimedians

anchor.snippet: The two bimedians of a convex quadrilateral are the line segments that connect the midpoints of opposite sides

annotations.note: Mathlib has no bimedian definition.

annotations.label: Maltitudes

anchor.snippet: The four maltitudes of a convex quadrilateral are the perpendiculars to a side

annotations.note: Mathlib has no maltitude definition.

anchor.snippet: The area can be expressed in trigonometric terms as

annotations.note: No quadrilateral area formula is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.note: No orthodiagonal-quadrilateral area formula is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bretschneider's formula

anchor.snippet: expresses the area in terms of the sides and two opposite angles

annotations.label: Reduction to Brahmagupta's formula

anchor.snippet: This reduces to Brahmagupta's formula for the area of a cyclic quadrilateral

annotations.note: Brahmagupta's area formula is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area from sides and two angles

anchor.snippet: Another area formula in terms of the sides and angles

annotations.note: No such quadrilateral area formula is in Mathlib.

annotations.note: No cyclic-quadrilateral area formula is in Mathlib.

annotations.note: No parallelogram area formula of this form is in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: Alternatively, we can write the area in terms of the sides and the intersection angle

annotations.note: No such area formula is in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: In the case of a parallelogram, the latter formula becomes

annotations.note: No such area formula is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area including sides and midpoint distance

anchor.snippet: Another area formula including the sides

annotations.note: No such area formula is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Last trigonometric area formula

anchor.snippet: The last trigonometric area formula including the sides

annotations.note: No such area formula is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area formula for concave quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: which can also be used for the area of a concave quadrilateral

annotations.note: No concave-quadrilateral area formula is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area from sides and diagonals

anchor.snippet: The following two formulas express the area in terms of the sides

annotations.note: No such area formula is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reduction to Brahmagupta's formula

anchor.snippet: The first reduces to Brahmagupta's formula in the cyclic quadrilateral case

annotations.note: Brahmagupta's formula is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area from bimedians and diagonals

anchor.snippet: The area can also be expressed in terms of the bimedians

annotations.note: No bimedian-based area formula is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area determined by three of bimedians/diagonals

anchor.snippet: suffice for determination of the area

annotations.note: Not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area via cross product of diagonals

anchor.snippet: The area of a quadrilateral ABCD can be calculated using vectors

annotations.note: Mathlib has an area form for vectors but no quadrilateral cross-product area formula.

annotations.label: Cartesian form of vector area

anchor.snippet: In two-dimensional Euclidean space, expressing vector AC as a free vector

annotations.note: No Cartesian quadrilateral-area formula is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trapezoids with perpendicular diagonals

anchor.section: Properties of the diagonals in quadrilaterals
anchor.snippet: The most general trapezoids and isosceles trapezoids do not have perpendicular diagonals

annotations.note: These shape types and their diagonal properties are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kite diagonal bisection

anchor.section: Properties of the diagonals in quadrilaterals
anchor.snippet: In a kite, one diagonal bisects the other

annotations.note: The perpendicular-bisector orthogonality lemma underlies this, but kites and the bisection statement are not formalized.

annotations.label: Diagonal lengths via law of cosines

anchor.snippet: The lengths of the diagonals in a convex quadrilateral ABCD can be calculated using the law of cosines

annotations.note: Mathlib's law of cosines is the tool used to compute diagonal lengths, but the quadrilateral diagonal-length formula itself is not stated.

annotations.label: Symmetric formulas for diagonal lengths

anchor.snippet: Other, more symmetric formulas for the lengths of the diagonals

annotations.note: These diagonal-length formulas are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler's quadrilateral theorem

anchor.section: Generalizations of the parallelogram law and Ptolemy's theorem
anchor.snippet: the sum of the squares of the four sides is equal to the sum of the squares of the two diagonals plus four times the square of the line segment connecting the midpoints of the diagonals

annotations.note: Mathlib has the parallelogram law (the special case where the midpoint segment vanishes) but not the full Euler quadrilateral identity.

annotations.label: Bretschneider's generalization of Ptolemy's theorem

anchor.section: Generalizations of the parallelogram law and Ptolemy's theorem
anchor.snippet: the following generalization of Ptolemy's theorem , regarding the product of the diagonals in a convex quadrilateral

annotations.note: Mathlib proves Ptolemy's equality for cyclic quadrilaterals (the special case) but not the general cos(A+C) formula.

annotations.label: Law of cosines for a quadrilateral

anchor.section: Generalizations of the parallelogram law and Ptolemy's theorem
anchor.snippet: This relation can be considered to be a law of cosines for a quadrilateral

annotations.note: Mathlib has the triangle law of cosines, of which this is the generalization to quadrilaterals; the quadrilateral relation itself is not formalized.

annotations.label: Relation from feet of normals to diagonal

anchor.snippet: If X and Y are the feet of the normals from B and D to the diagonal

annotations.note: This metric relation is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Relation at diagonal intersection point

anchor.snippet: and where the diagonals intersect at E

annotations.note: This metric relation is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Shape determined by sides and one diagonal

anchor.snippet: The shape and size of a convex quadrilateral are fully determined by the lengths of its sides in sequence and of one diagonal

annotations.note: This rigidity statement is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cayley-Menger determinant relation

anchor.snippet: by the Cayley-Menger determinant

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Cayley–Menger determinant.

annotations.label: Angle bisectors form cyclic quadrilateral or concur

anchor.snippet: The internal angle bisectors of a convex quadrilateral either form a cyclic quadrilateral

annotations.note: This angle-bisector theorem for quadrilaterals is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Angle bisectors meeting on diagonals

anchor.snippet: if the angle bisectors of A and C meet on diagonal BD , then the angle bisectors of B and D meet on diagonal AC

annotations.note: This result is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bimedians

anchor.snippet: The bimedians of a quadrilateral are the line segments connecting the midpoints of the opposite sides

annotations.note: Mathlib has no bimedian definition.

annotations.label: Varignon parallelogram

anchor.snippet: are the vertices of a parallelogram called the Varignon parallelogram

annotations.note: Varignon's theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Concurrency of bimedians and diagonal-midpoint segment

anchor.snippet: are concurrent and are all bisected by their point of intersection

annotations.note: This concurrency result is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Length of bimedian connecting sides b and d

anchor.snippet: This is also a corollary to the parallelogram law applied in the Varignon parallelogram

annotations.note: Despite the parallelogram law being in Mathlib, this bimedian corollary (and the Varignon parallelogram) is not.

annotations.label: Bimedians via opposite sides and midpoint distance

anchor.snippet: The lengths of the bimedians can also be expressed in terms of two opposite sides and the distance

annotations.note: No such bimedian-length formula is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equal bimedians iff perpendicular diagonals

anchor.snippet: The two bimedians have equal length if and only if the two diagonals are perpendicular

annotations.note: This bimedian/diagonal equivalence is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Perpendicular bimedians iff equal diagonals

anchor.snippet: The two bimedians are perpendicular if and only if the two diagonals have equal length

annotations.note: This bimedian/diagonal equivalence is not in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: Euler also generalized Ptolemy's theorem

annotations.label: Isoperimetric theorem for quadrilaterals

anchor.snippet: the one with the largest area is the square

annotations.note: No isoperimetric theorem for quadrilaterals is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dual isoperimetric theorem

anchor.snippet: The dual theorem states that of all quadrilaterals with a given area, the square has the shortest perimeter

annotations.note: This dual isoperimetric theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Maximum area with given sides is cyclic

anchor.snippet: The quadrilateral with given side lengths that has the maximum area is the cyclic quadrilateral

annotations.note: This maximal-area characterization is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Maximum area with given diagonals is orthodiagonal

anchor.snippet: Of all convex quadrilaterals with given diagonals, the orthodiagonal quadrilateral has the largest area

annotations.note: This maximal-area characterization is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fermat point of a convex quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: the point inside a quadrilateral that minimizes the sum of distances to the vertices is the intersection of the diagonals

annotations.note: The Fermat-point result for quadrilaterals is not in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: The centre of a quadrilateral can be defined in several different ways

annotations.note: Quadrilateral centres are not defined in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: There can also be defined a quasinine-point center

annotations.label: Existence of cyclic quadrilateral with same sides

annotations.label: Skew quadrilateral

anchor.snippet: A generalization of ordinary quadrilaterals to non-planar figures is called a skew quadrilateral

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Polygon` works in any affine space but has no dedicated skew-quadrilateral notion.
